sources: Institute for Government MP retirement tracker
Wikipedia: List of MPs of the 2024 United Kingdom Parliament who have left the Commons or changed parties
snapshot.method: Manually curated from public reporting as of April 2026. Update when new announcements are made.
snapshot.scope: Currently focused on confirmed defections, suspensions, and explicit standing-down announcements since GE2024.
snapshot.licence: Compilation: CC-BY 4.0 (data points are from public reporting; this aggregation is editorial).

gorton-and-denton.reason: Andrew Gwynne suspended (Whatsapp messages 2025) → resigned from Parliament 23 Jan 2026 citing ill health; by-election 26 Feb 2026 won by Green Party. Treat as fresh open seat.

runcorn-and-helsby.reason: Mike Amesbury suspended after assault conviction; resigned. By-election 1 May 2025 won by Reform UK (Sarah Pochin). Treat as fresh open seat.

islington-north.reason: Jeremy Corbyn won as Independent in 2024 with 49.2% (15,977 votes). High personal vote — bypass the 8% Independent ceiling.

rochdale.reason: Paul Waugh (Lab/Co-op) won 2024; George Galloway's Workers Party + 2024 Indep dynamic still active here. Bypass Indep cap.

blackburn.reason: Adnan Hussain won as Independent 2024 (Gaza-Independent dynamic). High-personal-vote case.

leicester-south.reason: Shockat Adam won as Independent 2024 (Gaza-Independent dynamic). High-personal-vote case.

dewsbury-and-batley.reason: Iqbal Mohamed won as Independent 2024 (Gaza-Independent dynamic). High-personal-vote case.

birmingham-perry-barr.reason: Ayoub Khan won as Independent 2024 (Gaza-Independent dynamic). High-personal-vote case.
